response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack credentials, GIS exposure via GISCollective, and low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby using DLang. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ (or Rust) for routing tile pipelines, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ work at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and no Rust. No demonstrated experience with navigation guidance systems or routing tile production pipelines either. The geospatial background and distributed/low-latency systems experience give partial alignment, but the core language and domain gap is significant.
strengths: Geospatial platform experience via GISCollective with production GIS deployments
Low-latency distributed systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of requests/sec
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, testing tooling authorship
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience (last meaningful C/C++ work ~2015) and no Rust
No navigation guidance or routing tile pipeline background
Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js, not systems-level performance engineering
